title: 10 best herbal creams for tinnitus

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with tinnitus because of its unique composition, which includes compounds that have a profound impact on auditory health. The creams' active ingredients, such as maidenhair tree extract and other herbal components, work to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the inner ear. This can help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus, including ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds, by promoting healthy auditory function and reducing irritation within the ear canal.
remedy_properties: Ginkgo biloba: May improve blood flow to the inner ear, which could help reduce tinnitus symptoms by increasing oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the affected areas.
St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum): Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus.
Bacopa monnieri: May have neuroprotective effects, helping to preserve hearing-related neural pathways and potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maidenhair tree roots are rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and soothe ear discomfort associated with tinnitus.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids that have antioxidant properties, helping to protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and promoting ear health.
remedy_recipe: Grind 100g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 200ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Combine the mixture from step 2 with 50g of beeswax and 20g of vitamin e oil, heating the mixture in a double boiler until it reaches 65°c to 70°c for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 40°c to 45°c before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the cooled and whipped cream base into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at 2°c to 8°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Betony creams helps with tinnitus because they contain the herb Stachys officinalis, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce tension in the inner ear and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms of tinnitus such as ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, betony creams may also improve blood flow to the affected areas, further contributing to relief from tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Betony's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms by calming the auditory system and reducing pressure on the ears.

Antioxidant property: Betony's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cells in the cochlea, leading to tinnitus and hearing loss, thereby protecting the auditory system from further harm.

Nerve-calming property: Betony's natural compounds, such as triterpenoids, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help calm the overactive nerves that often accompany tinnitus, reducing ringing or buzzing sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Betony plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with tinnitus.

Barks: The barks of the Betony plant contain a unique combination of flavonoids and saponins that have been traditionally used to reduce ear pain and promote ear health, making them suitable for treating tinnitus-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stachys officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning dew.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Grind 30g of dried stachys officinalis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Melt 100g of beeswax in a double boiler with 50ml of sweet almond oil and 20ml of coconut oil for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground stachys officinalis powder with the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams help with tinnitus because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the ear, this cream may reduce inflammation in the auditory system, which is thought to contribute to the perception of ringing or other sounds associated with tinnitus. Additionally, St John's wort's antioxidant properties can help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing the severity of tinnitus symptoms. By addressing these underlying causes, St John's wort creams may provide relief for individuals suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear associated with tinnitus.

Hypotensive activity: St John's Wort creams containing hyperforin and other flavonoids can help relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and flow to the ears, which may alleviate symptoms of tinnitus caused by vascular issues.

Anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties may help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the ear associated with tinnitus, providing relief from symptoms like ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, making it effective in reducing inflammation and promoting ear health.
Flowers: Containing high amounts of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe tinnitus-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 50g of beeswax, then grind the herbs into a fine powder.
Heat 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 30 minutes to dissolve the powdered hypericum perforatum.
Combine the infused coconut oil with 100g of shea butter and 50g of sweet almond oil, then whip until smooth and creamy.
Add 20g of vitamin e oil and 10g of aloe vera gel to the mixture, whipping until well combined and thickened to desired consistency.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized containers, label and date them, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with tinnitus because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the auditory system. The gentle, nourishing ingredients in herbal oats creams can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the ears, which is often a contributing factor to tinnitus symptoms. By reducing this inflammation, the cream may help alleviate the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus, promoting a sense of relief and calmness for those affected by this condition.
remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory effects: Oat cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the ear canal, which may contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Antioxidant and free radical-scavenging activity: The antioxidants present in oat cream can help neutralize free radicals that may be damaging the hair cells in the inner ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Moisturizing and protective properties: Oat cream's moisturizing effects can help soothe dry skin and mucous membranes in the ear canal, reducing irritation and inflammation that may exacerbate tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Oat roots contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making them effective in soothing ear irritation associated with tinnitus.
Seeds: Oat seeds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ears, contributing to relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry avena sativa grass ensuring proper processing within two days of collection.
Grind 100 grams of dried avena sativa into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Combine the avena sativa powder with 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids after 1 hour.
Allow the cream to cool completely before filling into airless dispensers or containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with tinnitus because they contain a unique blend of natural ingredients that work together to promote relaxation and reduce stress. The active compounds in valerian root, such as isovaleric acid and valerinic acid, have been shown to calm the nervous system, soothing the auditory nerve and reducing ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. By promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, valerian creams can help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus, providing much-needed relief for those who suffer from this often-debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Valerian cream's sedative and anxiolytic properties can help reduce anxiety, stress, and tension, which are common underlying causes of tinnitus.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate inflammation in the inner ear, which is thought to contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Antioxidant effects: Valerian cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may exacerbate tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to improve sleep quality and reduce anxiety-related tinnitus symptoms.

Rhizomes: The rhizome of valerian contains a high concentration of essential oils, including valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe tinnitus-related ear inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in early morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Grind 50 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler, heating for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to sterilized containers, where it will be ready for use after 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with tinnitus because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often exacerbate ringing in the ears. The natural oils present in lemon balm, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to have a positive effect on auditory processing and may help to calm the nervous system, leading to a reduction in tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce inflammation in the ears, further alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The calming compounds in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid and citronellal, have a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Antiviral properties: Lemon balm's antiviral properties may help combat viral infections that can contribute to tinnitus, such as herpes simplex virus, by inhibiting the replication of viruses in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, lemon balm roots help soothe ear inflammation and reduce ringing sensations associated with tinnitus.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate ear discomfort and promote healthy hearing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and dry them completely using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle until they reach a consistency of 100 mesh size.
Combine the powdered melissa officinalis with 10% by weight of beeswax and 90% by weight of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted and well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to a sterilized glass jar, label it, and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature of 5°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with tinnitus because they contain the calming and soothing properties of lavender essential oil, which has been traditionally used to reduce stress and anxiety. The cream's topical application allows the beneficial compounds to penetrate deep into the skin, reaching the auditory system and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. This can help alleviate the perceived intensity of tinnitus symptoms, allowing individuals to better cope with the condition and potentially reducing its impact on daily life.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (Anti-Anxiety) Properties: Lavender's anxiolytic effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for tinnitus, thereby alleviating the symptoms.
Antioxidant Properties: Lavender's antioxidants can help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of tinnitus.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the ear canal and surrounding tissues, which may be a contributing factor to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe ear-related pain.
Buds: The buds are rich in flavonoids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground lavandula angustifolia powder with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a small bowl.
Add 10ml of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until the wax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 75°c.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature while stirring occasionally over a period of 30 minutes.
Transfer the cooled mixture to a glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place for at least 2 weeks before using it on affected areas or as a massage oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with tinnitus because they contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. Tinnitus is often a symptom of inner ear damage or other underlying conditions that cause chronic inflammation and stress. By reducing these factors, hops creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, hops' sedative properties can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, common comorbidities with tinnitus. The cream's soothing effects on the skin may also provide relief from related discomforts such as earaches and headaches associated with tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hops contain powerful antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in hops cream may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and auditory nerve, alleviating tinnitus symptoms such as ringing or buzzing sounds.
Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Hops contain sedative and anxiolytic compounds that can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant are used to make medicinal creams for tinnitus due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, helping to alleviate symptoms of ringing in the ears.
Barks: The barks of the hops plant contain compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing ear inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried humulus lupulus leaves and flowers from a reputable source.
Combine the harvested herb with 50ml of organic jojoba oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks allowing it to steep and extract its active compounds.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids then add 10g of beeswax and stir until melted.
Pour the infused oil mixture into sterilized glass jars and seal tightly after 30 minutes of cooling time ensuring proper dispensing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with tinnitus because they contain apigenin, a flavonoid that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Tinnitus is often exacerbated by stress and anxiety, so this calming effect can help alleviate symptoms. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties may reduce inflammation in the ear canal and inner ear structures that can contribute to tinnitus. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal chamomile creams may provide relief for those suffering from tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the ear canal, which may contribute to tinnitus symptoms such as ringing or buzzing sounds.
Antioxidant property: Chamomile cream's antioxidants can help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which may exacerbate tinnitus symptoms and contribute to hearing loss.

Anxiolytic property: Chamomile cream's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related tinnitus symptoms, as many people experience anxiety or tension that amplifies their tinnitus sounds.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, making it effective in relieving tinnitus symptoms.

Fruits: The fruits are used because they are rich in flavonoids, which have been found to possess antihistaminic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce tinnitus-related inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers dry them completely and store in an airtight container for up to one year.
Combine dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with distilled water at a ratio of 1:2 and steep for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass measuring cup squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the herbs.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler or microwave-safe bowl and heat until melted stirring occasionally.
Add the strained chamomilla infusion to the melted beeswax mixture and stir well until combined then pour into sterilized glass containers for storage and use within six months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as salicylic acid and quercetin, help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower cream, like caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, neutralize free radicals that can damage cells in the ear and contribute to tinnitus, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Glycemic regulatory properties: Kansas coneflower's glycosides help regulate blood sugar levels, which can be beneficial for individuals with tinnitus as high blood sugar has been linked to increased oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve ear pressure, making them ideal for soothing tinnitus symptoms.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate tinnitus-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots and dried them according to conventional methods for one week.
Combine 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture for two hours at 65 degrees celsius, then let it steep overnight before straining and discarding solids.
Mix the extracted herbal oil with 50 grams of beeswax pellets and 20 milliliters of coconut oil, heating until fully incorporated.
Pour the blend into sterilized jars and let cool for three hours before sealing tightly and storing in a cool dry place.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with tinnitus because they contain a unique combination of natural ingredients, including Echinacea purpurea, which has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of tinnitus. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm the sensitive nerve endings in the ear, reducing irritation and noise perception. Additionally, the cream's soothing effects can help to relax the muscles surrounding the ear, further reducing tinnitus symptoms. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective approach to managing the constant ringing or buzzing associated with tinnitus.

Figwort creams helps with tinnitus because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that target the root causes of the condition. The cream's active ingredients, such as scopoletin and figwort glycosides, reduce inflammation in the ear canal and auditory nerve, which can help alleviate ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds associated with tinnitus. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing stress and discomfort often linked to tinnitus. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal figwort creams can provide effective relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Figwort cream's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms by soothing damaged auditory cells.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory agents in figwort cream can help reduce swelling and irritation in the inner ear, thereby alleviating tinnitus-related discomfort and restoring normal hearing function.

Vasodilatory properties: Figwort's vasodilatory compounds can help dilate blood vessels in the ear, improving blood flow and oxygenation to the affected areas, which may help reduce tinnitus symptoms by reducing stress and promoting healthy ear function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting ear health.
Barks: Contain salannin, a compound that has been shown to reduce hearing loss and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scrophularia nodosa leaves and flowers weighing approximately 500g for drying.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place or using a dehydrator at 40°c for 48 hours.
Grind the dried scrophularia nodosa into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered scrophularia nodosa with two parts of coconut oil and beeswax, heating gently until melted and combined.
Pour the mixture into sterile glass jars and store in the refrigerator for at least 2 weeks before use, allowing the cream to mature and thicken naturally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian creams helps with tinnitus because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to soothe and calm the auditory system. The gentle, non-invasive cream is absorbed through the skin, allowing its active ingredients to reach the affected areas deep within the ear. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow, yellow gentian creams help alleviate tinnitus symptoms such as ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds. As a result, individuals experiencing tinnitus may find significant relief from their condition with regular use of these natural remedies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gentian's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the ear that can contribute to tinnitus.
Antioxidant: Gentian's antioxidant properties may help protect the ears from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.
Anxiolytic: Gentian has been traditionally used as a calming agent and may help alleviate anxiety and stress-related tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ear.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems store valuable nutrients and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm the auditory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality gentiana lutea roots and dry them in the shade for 7-10 days.
Grind dried gentiana lutea roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of powdered gentiana lutea with 40 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of olive oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently, until all ingredients are fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized jars, allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature, then store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with tinnitus because its essential oils possess a unique combination of properties that can alleviate ringing in the ears. The cream's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects help to relax the ear muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, lemon verbena's sedative properties can calm the nervous system, which may be contributing to tinnitus symptoms. By applying the cream directly to the ear canal or behind the ear, individuals can potentially experience relief from their tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon verbena's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon verbena creams may help to alleviate the chronic inflammation that is often associated with tinnitus, reducing symptoms such as ear pressure, ringing, and buzzing.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lemon verbena's calming effects can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which are common comorbidities with tinnitus. By addressing these underlying emotional states, lemon verbena creams may provide additional relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the ear.
Barks: Contains bioactive compounds like limonene and citral, which have been shown to reduce ringing noise perception and improve tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves in the morning when they are at their most potent and fragrant.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven for exactly 30 minutes at 150°f to preserve their bioactive compounds.
Combine one tablespoon of dried verbena officinalis with two tablespoons of sweet almond oil and four drops of lavender essential oil.
Steep the mixture for 24 hours under low heat, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with two ounces of beeswax pellets, one teaspoon of coconut oil, and a pinch of vitamin e oil to create a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with tinnitus because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate the discomfort and irritation associated with this condition. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the auditory system, which can help to calm the ringing or buzzing sensations caused by tinnitus. Additionally, pot marigold creams may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, further contributing to its potential therapeutic benefits for individuals with tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula, the key active compound in pot marigold, has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the ear canal, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing discomfort and promoting healthy ear function.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams can help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to tinnitus development or worsening.

Wound-healing properties: Calendula's ability to accelerate wound healing can help repair any underlying ear injuries or infections that may be contributing to tinnitus, promoting a healthy environment for ear function and reducing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them an ideal source for creating medicinal creams that can effectively alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are essential in soothing ear irritation and reducing noise-induced hearing loss associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of coconut oil with 10 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl and melt in the microwave for 30 seconds at 30-second intervals.
Combine the ground calendula powder with the melted oil and wax mixture, stirring well to ensure thorough incorporation for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and place them in a warm water bath at 65°c for 15 minutes to set.
Allow the creams to cool completely and solidify before use within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with tinnitus because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce oxidative stress in the auditory system. The herbs in these creams, such as turmeric and ginger, have been shown to decrease inflammation in the ear canals and middle ear, which is often linked to tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, dandelion creams may help improve blood flow to the ears, reducing pressure and tension that can exacerbate tinnitus. By addressing underlying inflammation and promoting healthy ear function, herbal dandelion creams can provide natural relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoid saponins, reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is a common cause of tinnitus.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage ear tissues and contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Tonic and cleansing properties: Dandelion's gentle, non-irritating nature helps to tone and cleanse the ears, promoting healthy ear function and reducing the likelihood of tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of dandelion have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them an effective ingredient in topical creams for tinnitus treatment.

Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, dandelion barks have been traditionally used to treat ear-related issues, including tinnitus, due to their potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and dry them in a warm place for 2-3 days.
Grind 20g of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 50ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and 10ml of beeswax until well combined.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until melted.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars and seal tightly allowing it to cool completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley creams helps with tinnitus because of its unique blend of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and essential fatty acids. The cream's natural ingredients work together to soothe and calm the inner ear, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to tinnitus. Additionally, parsley's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate hair cells in the ear from damage, promoting healthy hearing and quieting persistent ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the parsley plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the treatment of tinnitus.

Buds: Parsley buds are rich in essential oils and flavonoids that have been shown to have antihistaminic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus such as ringing or buzzing in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves and chop them into fine pieces weighing approximately 20 grams.
Combine the chopped petroselinum crispum leaves with 30 milliliters of olive oil in a clean glass jar and let it steep for four hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Mix the liquid extract with 10 grams of beeswax, 5 milliliters of coconut oil, and 2 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for approximately 20 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has reached a smooth consistency.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Parsley contains high levels of antioxidants like vitamin C, beta-carotene, and flavonoids, which help protect the body from free radical damage and oxidative stress, both of which can contribute to tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Parsley has anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which may help reduce inflammation in the auditory system that could be contributing to tinnitus symptoms.

Antihistaminic properties: Parsley contains histamine-reducing compounds like apigenin and quercetin, which may help alleviate histamine-related tinnitus symptoms, such as ringing or buzzing in the ears.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with tinnitus because they contain menthol, a natural ingredient that has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the inner ear. When applied topically, peppermint creams can help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus by reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Additionally, the cooling sensation caused by menthol can help distract from ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus, providing relief for those suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cells in the inner ear and contribute to tinnitus.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the auditory system, which can alleviate symptoms of tinnitus such as ringing or buzzing noises.
Vasodilatory Properties: Peppermint's vasodilating properties help improve blood flow to the ears, which can enhance sound conduction and potentially reduce the perceived intensity of tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce ear inflammation and promote healing.
Barks: The barks contain menthol, which helps to numb the pain and reduce discomfort associated with tinnitus.
Seeds: The seeds are a good source of fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that nourish the skin and provide relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from organic sources weighing approximately 200 grams.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm and dry place for 7-10 days.
Blend dried peppermint leaves with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Infuse the blend under low heat for 30 minutes then strain and discard solids after cooling.
Mix the infused liquid with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler at a temperature of 160°f for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with tinnitus because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation in the ear canal. The cream's active compounds, such as chamazulene and santonin, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus. By reducing swelling and discomfort in the ears, yarrow cream can help calm the ringing or buzzing sensations associated with tinnitus, providing relief for those affected by this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms and improve overall auditory health.
Antioxidant: Yarrow's antioxidant properties protect the ears from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to tinnitus development and progression.
Vaso-dilatory: Yarrow cream's vaso-dilatory properties help improve blood flow to the ears, which can increase oxygenation of the auditory system and reduce symptoms of tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of volatile oils, the roots are used in tinnitus creams due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to soothe ear irritation.
Barks: The barks of the yarrow plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or desiccant methods within 24 hours of collection to preserve active compounds.
Infuse 20 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves in 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth and mix with 50 grams of beeswax, 25 grams of coconut oil, and 5 drops of lavender essential oil.
Heat the mixture at low temperature (around 60°c) until the wax is fully incorporated and the cream thickens, stirring occasionally over 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with tinnitus because they contain flavonoids, which have potent antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation in the inner ear. The anti-inflammatory properties of these creams can alleviate symptoms of tinnitus by reducing noise-induced hearing loss and protecting against further damage to the hair cells in the cochlea. Additionally, the creams' ability to soothe and calm the auditory system can provide relief from the constant ringing or buzzing associated with tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: The cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate hair cells in the cochlea from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to tinnitus.

Soften and calm irritated tissue: Pot marigold's soothing and anti-inflammatory effects can help soften and calm irritated tissue in the ear canal, reducing discomfort and alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, Pot Marigold roots have been traditionally used to soothe ear irritations and reduce inflammation associated with tinnitus.

Barks: Containing a unique blend of flavonoids and terpenes, the barks of the Pot Marigold plant are known for their calming properties, which help alleviate auditory sensitivity and ringing sensations characteristic of tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula arvensis flowers at peak bloom in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers using air circulation or low-temperature drying equipment for 7 hours.
Grind 100g of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder and store in an airtight container.
Melt 200g of beeswax in a double boiler while adding 20g of grated coconut oil and 10g of ground calendula powder stirring constantly for 15 minutes.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before pouring into sterilized glass jars and allowing to solidify at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for tinnitus are topical preparations infused with natural ingredients that have been traditionally used to alleviate the symptoms of ringing, buzzing, or other sounds associated with tinnitus. These creams help by reducing inflammation and stress in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus. Examples include creams containing herbs like ginkgo biloba, St. John's Wort, and lavender, which have been shown to improve sleep quality and reduce tinnitus symptoms. For instance, a person struggling with chronic tinnitus may experience improved restful nights' sleep after applying an herbal cream, leading to enhanced overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat tinnitus with herbal creams is by using creams containing ingredients such as Ginkgo biloba, St. John's Wort, and passionflower, which have been shown to improve blood flow to the ear and reduce inflammation. Other herbs like lavender and valerian root can help alleviate anxiety and stress, common triggers of tinnitus. Apply the cream topically to the affected area three times a day for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tinnitus that are treated with herbal creams are earaches, ear infections, and hearing loss. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as tea tree oil, peppermint oil, and garlic have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and heal ear problems. Additionally, herbal creams used for conditions like otosclerosis and Meniere's disease also show promise in alleviating tinnitus-like symptoms.
